content.text:
Clean build. Here's a summary of the refactored persistence layer:

### Architecture

```
storage.js          ← public async API (saveProject, loadProject, listProjects, deleteProject)
  ├── serialize.js        wraps project in versioned envelope
  ├── deserialize.js      validates + backfills old saves
  └── localStorageBackend.js  ← swappable backend (save/load/list/delete)

useAutosave.js      ← React hook, 5s debounce after last edit
```

### How to swap backends later

Call `setBackend()` with any object matching the interface:

```js
{
  save(projectId, name, serializedJson) → Promise<void>
  load(projectId) → Promise<json | null>
  list() → Promise<Array<{id, name, savedAt}>>
  delete(projectId) → Promise<void>
}
```

Serialize/deserialize stays the same regardless of backend — `storage.js` handles that layer.

### Changes made

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `localStorageBackend.js` | **New** — extracted localStorage logic into backend object |
| `storage.js` | **Rewritten** — async API, delegates to swappable backend |
| `useAutosave.js` | **New** — debounced autosave hook (5s after last edit, silent on failure) |
| `App.jsx` | **Updated** — async save/load/list, autosave wired in |
| `serialize.js` | Unchanged |
| `deserialize.js` | Unchanged |

# Fix Properties Panel X-Axis Overflow

## Context
The properties panel (`.panel` class, 280px wide) has content overflowing on the x-axis. Users have to scroll horizontally to see all content, which shouldn't happen. The panel should contain all its content within its fixed width.

## Root Causes
1. **`.panel` CSS** — has `overflow-y: auto` but no `overflow-x` rule, so x-overflow defaults to `visible`
2. **`InputField.jsx`** — the label has `flex: 0 0 80px` (fixed 80px) which combined with `gap: 8px`, padding, input, and suffix can exceed 280px minus panel padding. The input uses `flex: 1` but there's no `min-width: 0` to allow it to shrink below its content size (a flexbox gotcha)
3. **Inline `<select>` rows** in DoorProperties/WindowProperties have the same 80px label + flex layout

## Files to Change

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.module.css` | Add `overflow-x: hidden` to `.panel`, add `min-width: 0` utility |
| `src/ui/InputField.jsx` | Add `min-width: 0` on the input and its flex container so they shrink properly |

## Change 1: `PropertiesPanel.module.css`

In `.panel`:
```css
overflow-x: hidden;
```

## Change 2: `InputField.jsx`

On the outer flex container (the `<div>` with `flex: 1`), add `min-width: 0` so the flex child can shrink below its content width. Also add `min-width: 0` on the `<input>` itself.

## Verification
1. `npx vite build` — no errors
2. Open app, select a wall — all fields fit within the panel, no horizontal scroll
3. Switch to m units — longer decimal values still fit
4. Resize browser narrow — panel stays 280px, no overflow

## Context

## Root Causes
1. **`.panel` CSS** — has `overflow-y: auto` but no `overflow-x` rule, so x-overflow defaults to `visible`

3. **Inline `<select>` rows** in DoorProperties/WindowProperties have the same 80px label + flex layout

## Files to Change

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.module.css` | Add `overflow-x: hidden` to `.panel`, add `min-width: 0` utility |
| `src/ui/InputField.jsx` | Add `min-width: 0` on the input and its flex container so they shrink properly |

## Change 1: `PropertiesPanel.module.css`

In `.panel`:
```css
overflow-x: hidden;
```

## Change 2: `InputField.jsx`

On the outer flex container (the `<div>` with `flex: 1`), add `min-width: 0` so the flex child can shrink below its content width. Also add `min-width: 0` on the `<input>` itself.

## Verification
1. `npx vite build` — no errors
2. Open app, select a wall — all fields fit within the panel, no horizontal scroll
3. Switch to m units — longer decimal values still fit
4. Resize browser narrow — panel stays 280px, no overflow

# Fix Properties Panel X-Axis Overflow

## Context
The properties panel (`.panel` class, 280px wide) has content overflowing on the x-axis. Users have to scroll horizontally to see all content, which shouldn't happen. The panel should contain all its content within its fixed width.

## Root Causes
1. **`.panel` CSS** — has `overflow-y: auto` but no `overflow-x` rule, so x-overflow defaults to `visible`
2. **`InputField.jsx`** — the label has `flex: 0 0 80px` (fixed 80px) which combined with `gap: 8px`, padding, input, and suffix can exceed 280px minus panel padding. The input uses `flex: 1` but there's no `min-width: 0` to allow it to shrink below its content size (a flexbox gotcha)
3. **Inline `<select>` rows** in DoorProperties/WindowProperties have the same 80px label + flex layout

## Files to Change

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.module.css` | Add `overflow-x: hidden` to `.panel`, add `min-width: 0` utility |
| `src/ui/InputField.jsx` | Add `min-width: 0` on the input and its flex container so they shrink properly |

## Change 1: `PropertiesPanel.module.css`

In `.panel`:
```css
overflow-x: hidden;
```

## Change 2: `InputField.jsx`

On the outer flex container (the `<div>` with `flex: 1`), add `min-width: 0` so the flex child can shrink below its content width. Also add `min-width: 0` on the `<input>` itself.

## Verification
1. `npx vite build` — no errors
2. Open app, select a wall — all fields fit within the panel, no horizontal scroll
3. Switch to m units — longer decimal values still fit
4. Resize browser narrow — panel stays 280px, no overflow
